Class CapillaryDewPointFlash

java.lang.Object
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.flashops.saturationops.ConstantDutyFlash
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.flashops.saturationops.ConstantDutyTemperatureFlash
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.flashops.saturationops.CapillaryDewPointFlash
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, ConstantDutyFlashInterface, OperationInterface
public class CapillaryDewPointFlash extends ConstantDutyTemperatureFlash
Computes the capillary dew point temperature for multicomponent gas mixtures.

The capillary dew point accounts for the Young-Laplace pressure difference across the curved gas-liquid interface in a capillary or pore. The liquid phase pressure P_L differs from the vapor phase pressure P_V by the capillary pressure:

P_L = P_V - 2 * sigma * cos(theta) / r

where sigma is the interfacial tension, theta is the contact angle, and r is the pore radius. This shifts the dew point to higher temperatures compared to the bulk (flat-interface) dew point.

The algorithm uses the standard K-factor dew point iteration with a Poynting correction to account for the pressure difference between phases:

K_i_cap = K_i_bulk * exp(-Vm_L * deltaP_cap / (R * T))

Version:
1.0
Author:
experiment
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • CapillaryDewPointFlash

      public CapillaryDewPointFlash(SystemInterface system, double poreRadiusM)
      Constructor with default contact angle of 0 (perfect wetting).
      Parameters:
      system - the thermodynamic system
      poreRadiusM - the effective pore radius in meters, must be positive

    • CapillaryDewPointFlash

      public CapillaryDewPointFlash(SystemInterface system, double poreRadiusM, double contactAngleRad)
      Constructor with specified contact angle.
      Parameters:
      system - the thermodynamic system
      poreRadiusM - the effective pore radius in meters, must be positive
      contactAngleRad - the contact angle in radians (0 = perfect wetting)

  • Method Details

    • run

      public void run()
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable
      Overrides:
      run in class ConstantDutyTemperatureFlash

    • runSingleComponent

      private void runSingleComponent()
      Runs the capillary dew point calculation for single-component systems.

      Uses the bulk saturation point from BubblePointTemperatureNoDer and applies the linearized Kelvin temperature correction.

    • runMultiComponent

      private void runMultiComponent()
      Runs the capillary dew point calculation for multicomponent systems.

      Based on the standard dew point K-factor iteration (same algorithm as DewPointTemperatureFlash) with an additional Poynting correction term applied to all K-factors to account for the capillary pressure difference between phases.

    • computeKelvinShift

      private double computeKelvinShift()
      Computes the Kelvin shift term: Vm_L * deltaP_cap / (R*T).

      This value is subtracted from ln(K_i) to produce the capillary-corrected K-factor: ln(K_cap) = ln(K_bulk) - kelvinShift.

      Returns:
      the dimensionless Kelvin shift

    • getSurfaceTensionSafe

      private double getSurfaceTensionSafe()
      Gets the surface tension from the system's interphase properties, with a fallback value.
      Returns:
      surface tension in N/m

    • getLiquidMolarVolumeSafe

      private double getLiquidMolarVolumeSafe()
      Gets the liquid phase molar volume from the system, with a fallback value.
      Returns:
      liquid molar volume in m3/mol

    • getPoreRadius

      public double getPoreRadius()
      Gets the effective pore radius.
      Returns:
      pore radius in meters

      public void setPoreRadius(double poreRadiusM)
      Sets the effective pore radius.

      poreRadiusM - the pore radius in meters, must be positive
